title: Asok: The Modern Python Framework You Can Actually Read
content:
What if you could build modern web apps with **Next.js-style routing**, **Django-level features**, and **SvelteKit-like reactivity**... but with **zero dependencies** and code you can actually read and understand? Meet **Asok** тАФ a full-stack Python framework that challenges everything you thought you knew about web development. --- ## ЁЯОп The Identity: Modern Minimalism Asok isn't just another minimalist framework. It's a **batteries-included, modern framework** that happens to have zero external dependencies. While most frameworks force you to choose between: - **Bloated giants** with thousands of dependencies (Django, Rails) - **Empty shells** that require assembling 20+ libraries (Flask, Sinatra) - **Complex toolchains** with build steps and transpilers (Next.js, SvelteKit) **Asok gives you a third path**: A cohesive, modern framework built entirely on Python's standard library. --- ## ЁЯТО Zero Dependencies. Maximum Productivity. ```bash pip install asok asok create my-app cd my-app asok dev ``` That's it. No `npm install`. No vendor folders. No hidden complexity. Asok is built on **~11,000 lines of pure Python** тАФ small enough to read in an afternoon, powerful enough to ship production apps. **What you get out of the box:** - тЪб **File-based Routing** (`src/pages/` тЖТ URLs, just like Next.js) - ЁЯФР **Built-in Auth** (Sessions, CSRF, password hashing) - ЁЯЧДя╕П **AsokDB ORM** (Relations, migrations, automatic validation) - ЁЯОи **Admin Panel** (100% auto-generated, fully customizable) - ЁЯФД **Live Components** (Real-time UI via WebSockets тАФ no JS framework needed) - ЁЯМН **i18n & Localization** (Built-in translation engine) - ЁЯУз **Email Support** (SMTP integration included) - тП░ **Task Scheduler** (Cron-like background jobs) - ЁЯЪА **Production Ready** (WSGI/Gunicorn compatible, SELinux support) --- ## ЁЯЫгя╕П File-Based Routing: Because Your URLs Should Be Obvious Forget decorators. Forget `urls.py`. Your folder structure **is** your routing. ```plaintext src/pages/ тФЬтФАтФА page.html тЖТ / тФЬтФАтФА about/page.html тЖТ /about тФЬтФАтФА blog/[slug]/page.py тЖТ /blog/my-post тФФтФАтФА api/users/[id]/page.py тЖТ /api/users/123 ``` Dynamic routes? **Built-in.** ```python # src/pages/shop/[category]/page.py from asok import Request def render(request: Request): category = request.params.get('category') return f"Shopping in: {category}" ``` --- ## ЁЯза The Philosophy: Readable > Magical Most frameworks hide their complexity. Asok **exposes** it тАФ in the best way possible. - **No black boxes.** Every feature is implemented in readable Python. - **No magic imports.** You can trace every function call. - **No hidden runtime.** What you write is what runs. Want to know how auth works? Read `asok/session.py` (300 lines). Curious about the ORM? Check `asok/orm.py` (800 lines). Need to understand routing? Open `asok/core.py`. **This is code you can trust** тАФ because you can actually read it. --- ## ЁЯФе Real-Time by Default While other frameworks require Socket.IO, Django Channels, or complex WebSocket setups, Asok has **native Live Components**: ```python # src/components/Counter.py from asok import Component from asok.component import exposed class Counter(Component): count = 0 @exposed def increment(self): self.count += 1 def render(self): return f"""

Count: {self.count}

""" ``` ```html

Count: {{ count }}

``` **That's it.** Asok handles WebSocket connections, state synchronization, and UI updates automatically. --- ## ЁЯЧДя╕П AsokDB: The ORM That Gets Out of Your Way No migrations mess. No N+1 queries. Just clean, obvious data models. ```python from asok import Model, Field class User(Model): name = Field.String() posts = Relation.HasMany('Post') profile = Relation.HasOne('Profile') roles = Relation.BelongsToMany('Role') class Post(Model): author_id = Field.ForeignKey(lambda: User) author = Relation.BelongsTo('User') ``` Relations, validation, and automatic table creation тАФ **all included.** --- ## тЪЦя╕П Asok vs. The Giants | Feature | Asok | Flask | Django | |---|---|---|---| | **Dependencies** | **0** | ~6 | ~3 | | **Lines of Code** | ~11,000 | ~5,000 (core only) | ~270,000+ | | **Routing** | **File-based** | Decorators | `urls.py` | | **Admin Panel** | Auto-generated | Requires Flask-Admin | Heavy, legacy | | **Real-time** | **Native WebSockets** | Requires extensions | Django Channels | | **Philosophy** | Modern + Batteries | Micro | Mega | --- ## ЁЯЪА Production Ready, Zero Hassle Asok is **WSGI compatible** and production-tested: ```bash gunicorn wsgi:app --workers 4 ``` Special support for **RHEL/AlmaLinux (SELinux)** environments. Built-in **rate limiting**, **CSRF protection**, and **secure session handling**. --- ## ЁЯМН Join the Revolution Asok is for developers who believe: - **Less is more** тАФ but "batteries included" is non-negotiable - **Simplicity scales** тАФ complexity is a bug, not a feature - **You should understand your tools** тАФ frameworks should be readable **Get Started:** - ЁЯУЦ **Docs:** [asok-framework.com](https://asok-framework.com) - ЁЯТ╗ **GitHub:** [github.com/asok-framework/asok](https://github.com/asok-framework/asok) - ЁЯУж **PyPI:** `pip install asok` --- ## ЁЯТб The Bottom Line **Django** is a 15-year-old monolith. **Flask** is an empty canvas that requires assembly. **Asok** is a modern, complete framework you can actually understand. **~11,000 lines. Zero dependencies. Infinite possibilities.** Stop assembling. Start creating. --- #Python #WebDev #OpenSource #Minimalism #AsokFramework #ModernDevelopment #ZeroDependencies #CleanCode
